description: The Department for Business, Innovation and Skills (BIS) and the Department of Energy and Climate Change (DECC) have merged to form the Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy (BEIS). For information on education and skills, see the Department for Education (DfE) . For information on trade, see the Department for International Trade (DIT) .

details.body:
The Department for Business, Innovation and Skills (BIS) and the Department of Energy and Climate Change (DECC) have merged to form the Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy (BEIS).

For information on education and skills, see the Department for Education (DfE).

For information on trade, see the Department for International Trade (DIT).

details.body:
The Secretary of State is responsible for strategy and policy across the Department for Business, Innovation & Skills (BIS). The department’s main policy areas are:

  • business law
  • consumer issues
  • employment relations
  • enterprise and business support
  • trade policy
  • higher education
  • further education and skills
  • science
  • innovation

BIS was replaced by the Department for Business, Energy & Industrial Strategy in July 2016.

The Secretary of State responsibilities:

  • overall responsibility for the department, strategy and all policies
  • overall responsibility for the BIS budget
  • President of the Board of Trade
  • lead Cabinet minister for reducing regulatory burdens across government

ordered_roles.title: Secretary of State for Business, Innovation and Skills and President of the Board of Trade

details.body:
This is not a current government role.

The minister is responsible for further education, skills and lifelong learning. This role is shared between the Department for Business, Innovation and Skills and the Department for Education. Responsibilities include:

  • further education
  • adult Skills, Skills Funding Agency and skills strategy
  • lifelong learning and informal adult learning
  • apprenticeships
  • UK Commission for Employment and Skills and the Sector Skills Councils
  • Workplace Training reforms
  • qualifications reform programme

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for Further Education, Skills and Lifelong Learning

details.body:
The minister is responsible for:

  • business sectors (excluding construction and rail) and advanced manufacturing, including low carbon economy
  • enterprise
  • competiveness and economic growth, including economic opportunities and shocks
  • Business Bank and access to finance
  • Green Investment Bank
  • deregulation and better regulation
  • local and regional growth
  • export control
  • Royal Mail and the public data group
  • insolvency

Until May 2015 this role was known as the Minister for Business and Enterprise.

ordered_roles.title: Minister for Small Business, Industry and Enterprise

details.body:
The minister is responsible for:

  • intellectual property (including copyright, trademarks, patents, design, enforcement)
  • Co-chair of PBS (Professional and Business Services) council
  • cross-cutting EU engagement with Lord Livingston – each BIS Minister will lead on EU engagement on areas within their policy brief
  • all BIS business in the Lords – excluding trade/exports and science/higher education

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Business, Innovation and Skills and Minister for Intellectual Property

details.body:
The Permanent Secretary is the head of the department. The Permanent Secretary sits on the Departmental Board and chairs the Executive Board.

The Permanent Secretary has responsibility for overseeing the workings of the department, officials’ conduct and, as the Accounting Officer, the financial propriety of BIS.

ordered_roles.title: Permanent Secretary, BIS

details.body:
The Director General for Economics and Markets sits on the Executive and Departmental Boards. The Director General has responsibility for:

  • supporting BIS policy, strategy and delivery to help UK businesses
  • developing policies to promote the UK’s economic growth and looking after company law, financial reporting and corporate governance frameworks
  • ensuring that the UK’s trade and investment policy interests are well represented in international negotiations, and that export controls on military and dual use goods are robust
  • ensuring that the UK has a competitive, efficient and effective labour market to encourage business success and growth
  • promoting competitive markets for consumers and growth by using a range of competition and consumer policy interventions
  • Regional Growth Fund, UK devolution and economic shocks
  • promoting and defending the UK’s economic interests in and through the EU including the competitiveness strand of the Prime Minister’s referendum strategy, the single market and improving Whitehall performance on compliance with State Aid

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Economics and Markets, BIS

details.body:
The Chief Executive of UK Trade and Investment (UKTI) was accountable for developing and implementing exports and inward investment strategy on behalf of Her Majesty’s Government (HMG).

The Chief Executive:

  • provided clear leadership to UKTI and across Whitehall to help HMG meet the challenges and opportunities for UK exports and inward investment
  • chaired the senior cross-Whitehall Exports and Investment Board on behalf of the Secretary of State for Business, Innovation and Skills (BIS)
  • directly advised the Ministerial Exports Implementation Task Force

The Chief Executive sat on the BIS Executive and Departmental Boards, and the Foreign and Commonwealth Office (FCO) Management Board.

ordered_roles.title: Chief Executive, UK Trade & Investment

details.body:
The Director General for Business and Local Growth sits on the Executive and Departmental Boards and chairs the People Committee. The Director General has responsibility for:

  • leading BIS’ European Reform engagement
  • supporting local and regional growth
  • leading on BIS’ interactions with a range of sectors and individual key strategic companies across advanced manufacturing and services, digital economy, life sciences, and energy, materials and agritech

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Business and Local Growth, BIS

details.body:
The Chief Executive of the Shareholder Executive sits on the Departmental and Executive Boards.

The Chief Executive chairs the cross-Whitehall Central Spectrum Release Group, heads the Civil Service Government Corporate Finance Profession, and is an observer member of Cabinet Office Sub Committee PEX(A).

ordered_roles.title: Chief Executive, Shareholder Executive

details.body:
The Director General for Finance and Commercial sits on the Departmental and Executive Boards and chairs the Performance, Finance and Risk Committee. The Director General leads the Corporate Services Executive and is responsible for all finance and commercial issues. Further responsibilities include:

  • leading work on BIS’ public bodies reform
  • leading on the relationship with and governance of BIS’ partner organisations
  • chairing the Corporate Services Portfolio Board, Senior Remuneration Oversight Committee and the department’s Spending Moratorium

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Finance and Commercial, BIS

details.body:
The Director General for Enterprise and Skills, as well as Legal, sits on the Executive and Departmental Boards. The Director General has responsibility for:

  • enterprise
  • vocational education
  • better regulation and the Better Regulation Delivery Office
  • legal services

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Enterprise and Skills, and Legal, BIS

details.body:
The Director General for People, Strategy and Higher Education sits on the Executive and Departmental Boards. The Director General helps to provide management and leadership in the Department for Business, Innovation and Skills (BIS) and has responsibility for:

  • higher education
  • human resources
  • communications
  • change
  • BIS board and committee structures
  • departmental strategy including the growth agenda

ordered_roles.title: Director General, People, Strategy and Higher Education, BIS

details.body: BIS Board members provide the Department with strategic advice, support and challenge. They also attend Departmental Board meetings and meet with the Management Team.

ordered_roles.title: Non-executive board member, BIS

details.body: BIS Board members provide the Department with strategic advice, support and challenge. They also attend Departmental Board meetings and meet with the Management Team.

details.body: BIS Board members provide the Department with strategic advice, support and challenge. They also attend Departmental Board meetings and meet with the Management Team.

ordered_roles.title: Non-executive board member, BIS

details.body:
The minister is responsible for decentralisation and cities, a role shared between the Department for Business, Innovation and Skills and the Department for Communities and Local Government. Responsibilities include:

  • Big Society
  • planning policy

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for Decentralisation and Planning

details.body:
The minister’s responsibilties include:

  • Post Office and postal policy
  • employment relations including Acas (Advisory, Conciliation and Arbitration Service)
  • consumer policy and consumer affairs
  • competition policy
  • corporate governance
  • company law
  • social enterprise
  • Insolvency Service, including company investigations
  • BIS better regulation and efficiency and reform agenda

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Employment Relations and Consumer Affairs

details.body:
This is not a current government role.

The minister is responsible for:

  • further education
  • adult skills
  • Skills Funding Agency
  • skills strategy
  • lifelong learning
  • informal adult learning
  • apprenticeships
  • UK Commission for Employment and Skills
  • sector skills councils
  • workplace training reforms
  • qualifications reform programme
  • Enterprise and Regulatory Reform Bill

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Skills

details.body:
The Director General for Knowledge and Innovation sits on the Executive and Departmental Boards and chairs the Operations Committee.

The Director General is responsible for leading the group’s work to stimulate economic growth by making the UK world class at enabling and exploiting knowledge, research and innovation.

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Knowledge and Innovation, BIS

details.body:
BIS Board members provide the Department with strategic advice, support and challenge. They also attend Departmental Board meetings and meet with the Management Team.

The role of BIS’s lead non-executive board member is to:

  • support the Secretary of State in his role as Chair of the Board
  • liaise with the Department’s Non-Executive Directors and the government-wide lead non-executive board member
  • take an annual lead in reviewing the effectiveness of the Departmental Board.

ordered_roles.title: Lead non-executive board member, BIS

details.body: BIS Board members provide the Department with strategic advice, support and challenge. They also attend Departmental Board meetings and meet with the Management Team.

ordered_roles.title: Non-executive board member, BIS

details.body:
The minister is responsible for skills and enterprise. This role is shared between the Department for Business, Innovation and Skills and the Department for Education. Responsibilities include:

  • further education
  • adult skills, Skills Funding Agency and skills strategy
  • lifelong learning and informal adult learning
  • apprenticeships
  • UK Commission for Employment and Skills and the Sector Skills Councils
  • workplace training reforms
  • qualifications reform programme
  • small business, enterprise and access to finance
  • business support

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for Skills and Enterprise

details.body:
The BIS Chief Scientific Adviser is responsible for:

  • ensuring that the department’s policies are supported by the best science and engineering advice available
  • advising ministers and senior officials on science and engineering matters
  • acting as head of the science and engineering profession within BIS
  • working with the wider community of Chief Scientific Advisers to share good practice and resolve cross departmental problems

ordered_roles.title: BIS Chief Scientific Adviser

details.body: BIS Board members provide the Department with strategic advice, support and challenge. They also attend Departmental Board meetings and meet with the Management Team.

ordered_roles.title: Non-executive board member, BIS

details.body:
This role is shared between the Department for Business, Innovation and Skills (BIS) and the Department for Health (DH).

Responsibilities at the Department of Health include:

  • Cancer Drugs Fund
  • data and technology
  • genomics
  • medicines and industry
  • NICE and MHRA
  • regenerative medicine
  • research and development
  • specialised commissioning
  • uptake of new drugs and med tech

Responsibilities at BIS include:

  • agritech strategy
  • life sciences industrial strategy

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Life Sciences

details.body:
The minister is responsible for skills. This role is shared between the Department for Business, Innovation and Skills (BIS) and the Department for Education (DfE).

Joint BIS and DfE Responsibilities include:

  • further education - staffing, governance and accountability
  • localism in skills
  • pre-employment and basic skills
  • vocational qualifications
  • apprenticeships and traineeships
  • National Colleges
  • careers advice and guidance
  • Skills Funding Agency
  • employer ownership of skills

BIS responsibilities:

  • construction and rail business sectors
  • trade union and employment law
  • consumer and competition policy

DfE responsibilities:

  • raising the participation age (the age of compulsory participation in education or training until at least 18)
  • international education

From July 2014 to May 2015 this role included responsibility for Equalities. Before July 2014 this role was covered by the Minister of State for Skills and Enterprise.

ordered_roles.title: Minister of State for Skills

details.body: BIS Board members provide the Department with strategic advice, support and challenge. They also attend Departmental Board meetings and meet with the Management Team.

ordered_roles.title: Non-executive board member, BIS

details.body:
BIS

Responsibilities for BIS include:

  • intellectual property
  • EU/single market
    • single market in goods and services
    • digital single market (Lords lead)
    • Competitiveness Council
  • corporate governance, company law and Companies House
  • postal affairs and Post Offices
  • BIS better regulation
  • BIS corporate (including UKSBS)
  • BIS Lords business

DCMS

  • data protection, including sponsorship of the Information Commissioner’s Office
  • The National Archives (in the process of transferring from the Lord Chancellor)
  • nuisance calls

DCMS Lords business – excluding internet safety and security

ordered_roles.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for BIS and DCMS and Minister for Intellectual Property

details.body:
The Director General for Skills, Deregulation and Local Growth, as well as Legal, sits on the Executive and Departmental Boards. The Director General has responsibility for:

  • apprenticeships
  • vocational education
  • better regulation and regulatory delivery
  • local growth
  • legal services

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Skills, Deregulation and Local Growth, and Legal, BIS

details.body:
The Director General sits on the Executive and Departmental Boards and chairs the Digital and Data Committee.

The Director General is responsible for leading the group’s work to stimulate economic growth through research, innovation and commercialisation. He also leads on start ups, disruptive businesses and enterprise.

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Business and Science, BIS

details.body:
The Director General for Finance, Commercial and Digital Transformation sat on the Departmental and Executive Boards and chaired the Performance, Finance and Risk Committee. The Director General lead the Corporate Services Executive and was responsible for all finance, commercial and digital/IT issues. Further responsibilities included:

  • leading work on BIS’ public bodies reform
  • leading on the relationship with and governance of BIS’ partner organisations
  • a member of the cross-government Financial Management Review Board, leading on data and analytics
  • chairing the Senior Remuneration Oversight Committee

ordered_roles.title: Director General, Finance, Commercial and Digital Transformation, BIS

details.body:
The minister is responsible for trade and investment issues at the Foreign and Commonwealth Office (FCO) and the Department for Business Innovation and Skills (BIS). Responsibilities include:

  • trade policy
  • trade promotion and inward investment
  • UK Trade and Investment (UKTI)
  • UK Export Finance (UKEF)
  • member of Export Implementation Taskforce (chaired by the Secretary of State)

ordered_roles.title: Interim Minister of State for Trade and Investment
